full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic wind-turbine blade for a small-scale collegiate prototype turbine that must fit within a 45 cm cube envelope, operate at <= 48 VDC electrical bus, and survive 13 m/s continuous wind-tunnel inflow. The turbine is a 3-blade horizontal-axis upwind rotor; each blade is root-mounted to the hub via a single M8 steel stud through a central axial hole. Blade design constraints: rotor radius 220 mm (so two-blade-tip-to-tip diameter <= 450 mm within the 45 cm cube), root diameter 30 mm, tip chord 25 mm, root chord 60 mm, twist distribution 18 deg at root to 4 deg at tip, airfoil SG6043 (small-scale low-Re). Material: polycarbonate 3D print (FDM), E = 2.3 GPa, yield = 62 MPa, ultimate = 70 MPa, density = 1200 kg/m^3. Operating conditions: wind speed 13 m/s continuous (design point), tip-speed ratio lambda = 5.0 so rotor RPM = 2820 and centrifugal tip acceleration = (2 pi 2820 / 60)^2 x 0.22 = 19,200 m/s^2 = 1960 g at blade tip. Load cases: (R1) Centrifugal tensile load at 2820 RPM: blade self-weight approximately 30 g subjected to 1960 g at tip => resultant root-axial tension approximately 580 N; max von Mises at root <= yield / 2.0 = 31 MPa. (R2) Aerodynamic flap bending at design point: rated torque approximately 1.5 N*m divided by 3 blades = 0.5 N*m per blade, distributed along span gives root flap moment approximately 2.5 N*m; combined with R1, max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 41 MPa. (R3) Gust load case: 1.5 x design wind => 19.5 m/s gust, blade root flap moment scales as V^2 => 5.6 N*m; max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 47 MPa. (R4) Blade mass <= 35 g. (R5) Modal: first blade natural frequency (cantilevered at root) shall not coincide with 1P (rotor passing) frequency 47 Hz or 3P 141 Hz within +-10% bands; target first mode >= 170 Hz. (R6) Tip deflection at R3 <= 15 mm to avoid tower strike. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static with centrifugal body load + aero load) for R1-R3 + R6, modal for R5. Vibration secondary via R5. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242, blade root drawing, and a report listing mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, tip deflection at gust in mm, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic FDM-polycarbonate wind-turbine blade for a collegiate prototype turbine (3-blade, horizontal-axis, upwind rotor, 45 cm cube envelope, <= 48 VDC electrical bus, 13 m/s continuous wind-tunnel inflow). Each blade is root-mounted via a single M8 steel stud through a central axial hole; SG6043 airfoil, twist 18 deg root to 4 deg tip.

Envelope: rotor radius 220 mm (tip-to-tip diameter <= 450 mm), root diameter 30 mm, root chord 60 mm, tip chord 25 mm. Design point: tip-speed ratio lambda = 5.0, so rotor 2820 RPM (~1960 g at the tip).

Load cases.
- LC1 centrifugal tension at 2820 RPM: ~580 N root axial tension on a ~30 g blade.
- LC2 aerodynamic flap at design point: ~2.5 N*m root flap moment (rated torque ~1.5 N*m over 3 blades) combined with LC1.
- LC3 gust: 1.5 x design wind = 19.5 m/s, root flap moment scales with V^2 (~5.6 N*m); tip deflection <= 15 mm to avoid tower strike.
- LC4 modal: first blade cantilever mode must avoid 1P (~47 Hz) and 3P (~141 Hz) by +-10%.

prompt.title: Small-scale collegiate wind turbine blade (45 cm envelope)
prompt.brief: FDM-printed PC blade, 220 mm radius, 13 m/s design, 2820 RPM. Structural + modal qualification.

boundary_conditions.mounting: root M8 stud hole fully fixed
boundary_conditions.loads: centrifugal body force + equivalent distributed aero pressure

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero pre-reduced to equivalent spanwise pressure.
